---
title: "Professional Experience"
---

## Outsourcing Quilmes — NPS-Based Health Score (May 2022 – May 2023)
Role: Data Scientist
Scope: ~250,000 customers
Measurable impact: NPS 66 → 71 (+5 points) in 12 months
Context
Quilmes used NPS as its key customer satisfaction metric, but due to volume constraints it surveyed only around 10% of its customer base each month. This left the majority of customers without visibility and made it difficult to prioritize preventive actions.
Challenge

Measure “customer health” without surveys for most of the base.
Turn a statistical prediction into an operational, business-friendly indicator.
Integrate scattered signals (sales, logistics, customer support, app usage) into a single score.

Solution (what I built)
I designed a 0–100 Health Score for the entire portfolio, based on the probability of being a Promoter (NPS 9–10):

Trained a binary classification model (Logistic Regression) to estimate promoter vs. non-promoter probability.
Used model coefficients to build an interpretable, weighted score (0–100).
Defined actionable ranges (low / medium / high health) that triggered different strategies.

Implementation (how I did it)
Cross-functional data sources:

Sales: recency/frequency, volume variation, product mix, average ticket
Logistics: fulfillment, incidents, delays, delivery quality
Customer support: complaints, resolution time, recurrence
App & rewards: engagement, benefit adoption, usage habits

Interpretable model: Logistic Regression enabled clear explanations of what drives the score.
Business translation: I converted the prediction into a simple 0–100 score and defined thresholds together with end users to ensure actionability.
Results

NPS increased from 66 to 71 (+5) during May 2022 – May 2023.
The organization moved from working with a partial signal (10% surveyed) to full coverage with a per-customer score.
Improved prioritization: low-health customers received focused, timely corrective strategies.

Why this was key

Choosing an explainable model accelerated adoption: the business trusts what it understands.
Combining operational signals (logistics + support) had a real impact on satisfaction, beyond sales alone.

What to showcase in a portfolio (ideal)

A simple diagram: Sources → Features → Model → Health Score → Actions
An example of “Top drivers” (without sensitive data)
A before/after NPS chart plus the health score distribution

## PoC — Demand forecasting
- (placeholder)
